name: Cuautla

palette.name: Heroic Brick
palette.rationale: Brick red references Cuautla's independence memory and historic-center masonry without claiming an official color.

palette.name: Morelos Garden Green
palette.rationale: Warm-climate planting and irrigated gardens provide a visible everyday landscape layer.

palette.name: Sunlit Stucco
palette.rationale: Light stucco supports colonial walls, plazas, and bright Morelos daylight as a flexible neutral.

palette.name: Railway Iron
palette.rationale: A dark iron neutral nods to the old railway setting and gives the palette a practical anchor.

sources.title: Cuautla Oficial
sources.note: Used for official municipal and independence-history context.

sources.title: Zona de Monumentos Históricos en Cuautla
sources.publisher: Instituto Nacional de Antropología e Historia
sources.note: Used for historic monument, siege, and railway-adjacent heritage context.

notes: Standard starter record intended for later sourcing review; no official city colors are claimed.
editorialSummary: Cuautla's standard starter palette gives designers historic brick, garden green, sunlit stucco, and railway iron as cautious editorial layers for a heroic Morelos city identity.
